---
title: "Predicting the Eredivisie using a Bayesian Approach"
date: 2026-05-26
date-format: "D MMM YYYY"
format: 
  revealjs
---

## Introduction

- Predicting match outcomes

## About

- Eredivisie consists of $T = 18$ teams
  - Each team play twice against each other team
- There are $G = 306$ games

## Model

- The goals scored in the $g$-th game by the home and away team are:
$$y_{g,1}, \quad\text{and}\quad y_{g,2}$$

- These are counts, and can take values $0, 1, \ldots$, typically modelled as following a Poisson distribution
$$y_{g,j} \mid \theta_{g,j} \sim \mathrm{Poisson}(\theta_{g,j})$$ with the parameters $\theta_{g,j}$ representing the scoring intensity in the $g$-th game for the team playing at home ($j=1$) and away ($j=2$), respectively.

- The parameters assume a log-linear random effect model:
$$\begin{align}
\log\left(\theta_{g,1}\right) &= \eta + \alpha_{h[g]} + \delta_{a[g]} \\
\log\left(\theta_{g,2}\right) &= \alpha_{a[g]} + \delta_{h[g]} 
\end{align}$$

## Priors

We assume the following priors:
$$
\begin{align}
\eta &\sim \mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.0001) \\
\alpha_{t} &\sim \mathrm{Normal}(\mu_{\alpha}, \tau_{\alpha}) \\
\delta_{t} &\sim \mathrm{Normal}(\mu_{\delta}, \tau_{\delta})
\end{align}
$$

We specify identifiability constrains, i.e., sum-to-zero constraint:
$$\sum_{t=1}^{T}\alpha_{t} = 0, \quad \sum_{t=1}^{T}\delta_{t} = 0$$

We define the following hyperpriors:
$$
\begin{align}
\mu_{\alpha} &\sim \mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.0001), &\quad \mu_{\delta} &\sim \mathrm{Normal}(0, 0.0001) \\
\tau_{\alpha} &\sim \mathrm{Gamma}(0.1, 0.1), &\quad \tau_{\delta} &\sim \mathrm{Gamma}(0.1, 0.1)
\end{align}
$$

## Data

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import pymc as pm
import arviz as az
import pytensor.tensor as pt

DATA_PATH = Path("/home/robbin/Datasets/football/nl")
df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH / "20222023.csv", header=0)
df.head()
```

## Code

```{python}
home_idx, teams = pd.factorize(df["HomeTeam"], sort=True)
away_idx, _ = pd.factorize(df["AwayTeam"], sort=True)
coords = {"team": teams}

with pm.Model(coords=coords) as football_model:
    # Mutable data
    home_team = pm.Data("home_team", home_idx)
    away_team = pm.Data("away_team", away_idx)

    # Global model parameters
    home = pm.Normal("home", mu=0, sigma=1)
    sd_att = pm.HalfNormal("sd_att", sigma=2)
    sd_def = pm.HalfNormal("sd_def", sigma=2)
    intercept = pm.Normal("intercept", mu=0, sigma=1)

    # Team-specific parameters
    atts_star = pm.Normal("atts_star", mu=0, sigma=sd_att, dims="team")
    defs_star = pm.Normal("defs_star", mu=0, sigma=sd_def, dims="team")

    atts = pm.Deterministic("atts", atts_star - pt.mean(atts_star), dims="team")
    defs = pm.Deterministic("defs", defs_star - pt.mean(defs_star), dims="team")
    home_theta = pt.exp(intercept + home + atts[home_idx] + defs[away_idx])
    away_theta = pt.exp(intercept + atts[away_idx] + defs[home_idx])

    # Likelihood
    home_points = pm.Poisson("home_points", mu=home_theta, observed=df["FTHG"].values)
    away_points = pm.Poisson("away_points", mu=away_theta, observed=df["FTAG"].values)

pm.model_to_graphviz(football_model)
```

## Sample

```{python}
with football_model:
    football_trace = pm.sample(
        draws=2000, tune=2000, chains=4
    )

az.plot_forest(football_trace, var_names=["atts"])
```
